text: Fires represent a significant risk to the safety and infrastructure of modern smart cities. While early detection is critical to mitigating property damage and saving lives, traditional sensor-based systems often struggle with latency, limited coverage, and high false-alarm rates in complex urban environments. This paper introduces the Smart Fire Detection System (SFDS), an advanced real-time approach built upon the state-of-the-art YOLOv8 deep learning algorithm. The SFDS is integrated into a multi-layered smart city framework consisting of IoT, Fog, Cloud, and Application layers, designed to optimize data processing speeds and response times. Unlike existing models, the proposed system was trained on a comprehensive dataset of 26,520 images encompassing a diverse array of indoor and outdoor fire and smoke scenarios under varying lighting and noise conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the SFDS achieves state-of-the-art performance, reaching a high precision rate of 97.1% across all classes and a recall rate of 99.3% for smoke detection. By leveraging the speed and accuracy of YOLOv8, the system significantly reduces false alerts and provides a cost-effective, scalable solution for public safety, forest monitoring, and intelligent security infrastructures.
